title: acer rubrum f. microphyllum
intro: Acer rubrum f. microphyllum, also known as the Dwarf Red Maple, is a variety of the Red Maple tree (Acer rubrum) that is characterized by its compact growth habit and smaller leaves. The leaves of the Dwarf Red Maple contain salicylic acid, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties, which is also the active ingredient in aspirin. The medicinal properties of the Dwarf Red Maple include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, making it a potential natural remedy for conditions such as arthritis and headaches. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and salves, and its leaves can also be used to make a salicylic acid-rich syrup.
